customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, cooperative tone throughout. Primary uncertainty is practical: Carter is new to this home (less than two months), has no established bill history, and is unsure how the EV will affect usage. No skepticism or hostility signals — but legitimacy was not anchored in the intro, and Carter had to ask for the company name at 03:56.
unresolved_concerns: Carter's stated preference was weekends — weekday 4PM slot was accepted after clarification but without a verbal handshake confirming buy-in
Carter will not be at home on weekdays — computer/email access at 4PM is unconfirmed; closer should verify Carter can pull up the design on a screen before walking through it
No bill history established — home is less than two months old; closer must build the bill story from EV load and square footage, not from a stated average
Carter's mom is on the title — it is unclear whether she will be on the appointment; closer should confirm at the top of the call
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior home solar inquiry that did not proceed due to unspecified 'events' — Carter did not elaborate and Varun did not probe; closer should not press this unless Carter raises it
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will call Carter at 4PM tomorrow
Specialist will email a design during the call showing panel placement and cost comparison between ComEd and solar
EV will be included in the system sizing notes
closer_should_know: Carter has an EV and is one month into a new home with no bill history — the EV load is the primary bill-pain anchor available, and Carter volunteered it unprompted. Size the system to cover both home and EV charging and lead with that framing. Also confirm at the top of the call that Carter can access email on a screen (not just a phone) and that Carter's mom, who is on the title, is either present or has been consulted.
